title: Syncope

red_flags.text: Supports urgent cardiovascular assessment for ECG abnormality, heart failure, transient loss of consciousness during exertion, family history of sudden cardiac death, new or unexplained breathlessness, or a heart murmur.
red_flags.rationale: Supports mapped exertional TLoC, family sudden death, urgent referral, and cardiology follow-up.
red_flags.evidence_extract: Supports urgent cardiovascular assessment for ECG abnormality, heart failure, transient loss of consciousness during exertion, family history of sudden cardiac death, new or unexplained breathlessness, or a heart murmur.

examination.text: Supports event and witness history, posture, prodrome, appearance, movements, tongue biting, injury, duration, confusion, focal weakness, previous events, medical and family history, medicines, vital signs, lying and standing blood pressure, cardiovascular examination, and neurological signs.

source_limitations: Children, presyncope without loss of consciousness, chest-pain and palpitation red-flag labels, and fixed one-week review remain unsupported.
Diagnosis, risk classification, investigation selection, referral, and driving advice remain clinician-led and locally reviewed.
Qualified clinician review has not been completed.
All prior supported legacy-item mappings were removed because canonical item-level applicability and workflow-specific rationale were not present in explicit repository records.
curation_policy: Direct guideline curation: retain only explicitly mapped content; rewrite only with explicit item mapping; remove unmapped legacy content; add only committed exact-section evidence summaries.
